panels.description: Seconds since the last sample of core telemetry. Normally API/worker/scheduler should update within minutes; Beyla depends on recent traffic.
panels.title: Core Telemetry Freshness (Seconds Since Last Sample)

panels.description: Shows optional signals that are easily mistaken for "missing data". Faro/k6 at 0 means there are no matching events or load-test runs in the current time range; recording rules and Alloy are collection-pipeline health signals.
panels.title: Optional Signal Coverage

options.content:
### How to read these

- Prometheus only scrapes Alloy and Prometheus directly; API, worker, and scheduler metrics arrive via OTLP/Alloy remote write.
- Faro = 0 means there are no browser RUM events in the current time range; investigate frontend initialization or actual browser traffic.
- k6 missing = 1 means Prometheus currently has no recent k6 smoke/load metrics; they appear automatically after running a load test.
- scheduler_task_runs_total may currently be labeled under webhookwise-worker in production, so the panel judges freshness from the task metric itself.

panels.description: Faro RUM data comes from frontend browser instrumentation. If production shows 0, it means no RUM events reached Grafana Faro in the selected time range; it does not mean API/worker metrics have stopped being collected.
panels.title: Faro Frontend RUM Receive Volume

panels.description: k6 metrics only appear after a load test runs; No data or 0 usually means no smoke/load profile was executed in the selected time range. This intentionally uses range functions to read the most recent sample, avoiding misleading stale markers.
panels.title: k6 Smoke Test Results (Most Recent Run)

panels.description: Shows which environment and services the selected datasource is actually receiving. Useful when a public Grafana points at dev-like telemetry.
panels.title: Current Collection Environment & Services (Environment / Services)

panels.description: Current active HTTP requests by route. Payload sizes are tracked separately in Webhook Payload Size P95.
panels.title: HTTP Active Requests (Active Requests)
Queue Operation Duration P95 (Queue Operation Duration)

targets.expr: sum by (webhook_source, webhook_relation, webhook_suppressed) (rate(webhook_noise_evaluations_total[$__rate_interval])) or vector(0)

panels.title: AI Cost by Model (Cost by Model)

targets.expr: sum by (client, server, connection_type) (rate(traces_service_graph_request_total[$__rate_interval])) or vector(0)
sum by (client, server, connection_type) (rate(traces_service_graph_request_failed_total[$__rate_interval])) or vector(0)

panels.description: P95 duration of Loki write requests from Alloy to Loki.
panels.title: Loki Write Latency P95

panels.description: Write retries and dropped entries/bytes. Any sustained non-zero value deserves collector/Loki inspection.
panels.title: Loki Retries & Drops (Retries / Drops)
